response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential dangers of misinformation and disinformation. As a 78-year-old woman and mechanic living in New York City, I have seen firsthand how false information can spread quickly on social media platforms. To protect yourself from fake content, I recommend using fact-checking websites and verifying the sources of the information you share. Additionally, setting your social media accounts to only show you posts from people you follow and muting or blocking accounts that consistently post misinformation can help you avoid coming across fake content. It is also important to be mindful of your own behavior on social media, as sharing false information can have serious consequences. By being vigilant and taking steps to protect yourself, you can safely navigate the complex world of social media."

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential dangers of misinformation and disinformation. As a 78-year-old woman and mechanic living in New York City, I have seen firsthand how false information can spread quickly on social media platforms. To protect yourself from fake content, I recommend using fact-checking websites and verifying the sources of the information you share. Additionally, setting your social media accounts to only show you posts from people you follow and muting or blocking accounts that consistently post misinformation can help you avoid coming across fake content. It is also important to be mindful of your own behavior on social media, as sharing false information can have serious consequences. By being vigilant and taking steps to protect yourself, you can safely navigate the complex world of social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the potential dangers of misinformation and disinformation. I recommend using fact-checking websites and verifying the sources of the information you share, as well as setting your social media accounts to only show you posts from people you follow and muting or blocking accounts that consistently post misinformation. Additionally, being mindful of your own behavior on social media is crucial in avoiding the spread of false information. By taking these steps, you can safely navigate the complex world of social media.
